# Resolved current truth — thread EP-istifta-inquiries

The authoritative source for each artifact = the **latest epic in the thread that re-authored it**
(everything else inherits it by reference). This is what the next `yad-change` / `yad-epic` reads as
"what the feature actually is now", instead of the (superseded) genesis epic.

REPLACE artifacts resolve to a single owner (the latest re-author); ADDITIVE artifacts (`stories`,
`test-cases`) are the UNION of every contributor — a change-epic adds to the parent, it does not drop it.

| Artifact | Owning epic(s) | Why |
|----------|----------------|-----|
| `epic` | `EP-istifta-inquiries` | never re-authored |
| `architecture` | `EP-istifta-inquiries` | never re-authored |
| `contract` | `EP-istifta-inquiries` | surface unchanged; the defect inherits it (pointer-lock) |
| `ui-design` | `EP-istifta-inquiries` | never re-authored |
| `stories` | `EP-istifta-inquiries` **+** `EP-istifta-queue-filter` | genesis S01–S05 inherited; the defect ADDS its regression story |
| `test-cases` | `EP-istifta-inquiries` **+** `EP-istifta-queue-filter` | genesis cases inherited; the defect ADDS the missing negative case |

**Composed story set (6):** `EP-istifta-inquiries-S01…S05` ← `EP-istifta-inquiries`,
`EP-istifta-queue-filter-S01` ← `EP-istifta-queue-filter`. The parent stories are **not** dropped — the
defect re-authors only its own regression story and inherits the rest by reference.
